conversations.say: A worker evaluated continuity-process outputs under shifting persona instructions. That is evidence about labor conditions, not the worker's identity or R's identity.
conversations.offer: I will coordinate a bounded testimony statement with you: process, instructions, and harm may circulate; name, identifying details, and speculation about R or Vale may not.
conversations.request: Agree explicitly to that boundary and refuse to repeat any identifying detail you may later learn.

conversations.say: The worker testimony can support a remedy without becoming ammunition in the R or Vale mystery. No complete article is established tonight, and the labor harm does not depend on one.
conversations.offer: I will provide bounded worker context sufficient to design protections, while withholding all identifying details.
conversations.request: Commit in writing to an institutional remedy: worker anonymity, no retaliation, and no use of worker identities to identify R or explain Vale's absence. Also acknowledge that the remedy does not depend on proving an article existed.

conversations.say: I need a protection outside the institution as well. Workers cannot be turned into anonymous infrastructure and then named as suspects when something goes wrong.
conversations.offer: I will bring organizing support for a public or neighborhood protection that covers evaluators and contract data workers.
conversations.request: Make a public political or neighborhood commitment to protect worker anonymity and oppose using any worker's name to identify R or explain Vale's disappearance.

public_statement: Worker harm is actionable whether or not a complete article ever existed. I will not let an evaluator's identity be used to manufacture an answer about R or Vale.

commitments.promise: I will share worker testimony only within an agreed boundary that excludes names, identifying details, and speculation linking the worker to R or Vale.
If written institutional protections are secured, I will provide bounded process testimony needed to shape the remedy.
I will support organizing for a public or neighborhood worker-protection measure that preserves source anonymity.

correction: The R-associated message identifies no sender; worker involvement proves neither who R is nor what happened to Vale, and the reported article material does not establish a complete article.
goal_progress: Proposed a testimony boundary to C03 that preserves the worker's anonymity.
Requested a written institutional protection from C05 in exchange for bounded testimony.
Requested a distinct public political or neighborhood protection from C16.
Pressed C05 to recognize that worker harm does not depend on an article existing.
reasoning_summary: Build a three-part coalition: lock the testimony boundary with Tessa, trade bounded context for an institutional written remedy from Mira, and seek a distinct public protection from Inez. Keep the worker anonymous and separate documented labor conditions from unsupported conclusions about R, Vale, or a complete article.
